source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Switch S is thrown to position A as shown in Fig. 3.30. One second after the switch is thrown from A to B, it is finally thrown from B to C. What are the magnitude and direction of the currents in $R_{2}, R_{3}, R_{4}$, and $R_{5}$ just after the switch is thrown from B to C?

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Switch S is thrown to position A as shown in Fig. 3.30. One second after the switch is thrown from A to B, it is finally thrown from B to C. What are the magnitude and direction of the currents in $R_{2}, R_{3}, R_{4}$, and $R_{5}$ just after the switch is thrown from B to C?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

metadata.reasoning_chain: Just before $t=1\,\mathrm{s}$, $i_{3}(1-)=0.47e^{-0.5}=0.29\,\mathrm{A}$ (downward). Since the current in an inductor cannot change suddenly, $i_{3}(1+)=0.29\,\mathrm{A}$ (downward) and $i_{5}(1+)=0\,\mathrm{A}$. For the remainder of the circuit, $i_{2}(1+)+i_{4}(1+)=0.29\,\mathrm{A}$ and, with equal division, $i_{2}(1+)=i_{4}(1+)=0.145\,\mathrm{A}$ (upward).
